results.title: Cross-phase UAT audit for v1.0 verification debt
results.content: Audit on 2026-03-30 found 12 outstanding UAT items across phases 03, 04, and 05: 10 pending active tests and 2 server-blocked invoice tests. Phase 03 contract tests are active because contracts endpoints and conflict rules still exist in contract.controller/service. Phase 05 payment/debt tests are active because payment creation, overpayment conflict handling, and debt reporting endpoints/services still exist. Phase 04 blocked items are infrastructure-related: invoice tables come from migration 1774900000000, runtime uses DB_MIGRATIONS_RUN default false, .env.example does not document it, and no phase VERIFICATION.md files exist anywhere in .planning/phases, so milestone verification debt remains broader than UAT alone.

results.title: Phase 01.1 plan refinement from integration docs
results.content: Reviewed .planning/frontend-integration-contract.md and .planning/frontend-screen-api-map.md in rental-management-ui and refined the active Phase 01.1 planning artifacts instead of creating a new phase. Updated 01.1-CONTEXT to lock in runtime decisions for base URL normalization with or without /api, typed query filters like houseId/roomId/billingMonth, no-content delete handling, string-or-string[] error message preservation, transport-vs-presentation date/money boundaries, and visible PDF/CORS risk. Updated 01.1-03-PLAN so smoke coverage now explicitly tests login plus one authenticated follow-up request, 401 vs 403 behavior, JSON envelope parsing, binary PDF flow, base URL normalization, query serialization, empty-body deletes, and localized TODOs for unresolved payloads such as debt summary, move-in/out, room-service assignments, and asset logs. Also corrected frontend-screen-api-map.md to link to the local frontend integration contract file in the UI repo.

results.title: Phase 4.1 Wave 2 admin API-key recovery
results.content: Implemented Wave 2 for Phase 4.1 by adding scripts/reset_admin_api_key.py. The script resolves an admin by username, rejects missing or non-admin users, rotates the admin API key through AuthService.rotate_api_key, and records an admin.api_key_rotated audit event. Added tests/integration/test_phase4_1_admin_recovery.py covering successful rotation, invalidation of the old key, audit log creation, and rejection for missing/non-admin users. Also carried forward supporting auth/store layer changes needed for clean setup-wizard behavior: has_admin_users on the repository boundary and the dashboard login integration test adjustment so login-page assertions don't get redirected to /setup in admin-present scenarios. Verified with pytest over phase4.1 recovery/setup, phase4 admin HTTP, phase4 gateway e2e, and unit auth tests: 45 passed. Mypy and ruff also pass.

results.title: packageTourApi admin subscription list with company owners
results.content: Added GET /v1/admin/subscriptions in the plan_subscription module. Implemented GetAdminSubscriptionListUseCase with paged subscription loading from SubscriptionService, bulk company loading via ICompanyService.findBaseListId, and owner-user enrichment by resolving company users then filtering COMPANY_OWNER roles. Files: AdminSubscriptionController, SubscriptionService, SubscriptionServiceImpl, GetAdminSubscriptionListUseCase, GetAdminSubscriptionListUseCaseImpl, AdminSubscriptionFilterRequest, AdminSubscriptionPublicData.

results.title: Paused Phase 01.1 smoke coverage work
results.content: Paused rental-management-ui during Phase 01.1 Plan 01.1-03 after creating a WIP checkpoint commit. Added Vitest as the minimal smoke-test runner, created vitest.config.ts and a yarn test script, drafted shared API/session smoke tests under src/lib/api/__tests__/client.test.ts and src/lib/auth/__tests__/session.test.ts, patched src/lib/api/client.ts so protected 401 responses clear the stored access token, and wrote .planning/HANDOFF.json plus .planning/phases/01.1-api-integration-contract-and-typed-data-layer/.continue-here.md for resume context. The work is intentionally paused before running yarn test, so the next action is to run the new test suite, fix any mocking or assertion failures, then continue with contract-gap TODOs and final lint/build verification.
